objections.description: No explicit mapping provided for which of src/backend/ingest/, src/backend/search/, src/backend/dbsync/, src/backend/commitment/, web/, infra/, deploy/ go to src/backend vs src/frontend. This ambiguity guarantees broken imports and circular dependencies. For example, if src/backend/search/ imports from src/backend/ingest/ and they land in different directory trees with different Python path prefixes, the import graph breaks immediately.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide explicit mapping: src/backend/ingest/, src/backend/search/, src/backend/dbsync/, src/backend/commitment/ → src/backend/; web/ → src/frontend/; infra/ and deploy/ remain at root for Phase 1. Document cross-package dependencies before moving.

objections.description: MCP servers (mcp_server.py, mcp_es_server.py) are invoked via absolute paths in Claude Desktop configs, VS Code settings, and other MCP client manifests. Moving these files without simultaneously updating all client configurations will break integrations immediately upon deployment. The plan mentions 'validate after' which is too late.
objections.suggested_fix: Keep mcp_server.py and mcp_es_server.py at root in Phase 1. Create shims that delegate to src/backend/ only after verifying all MCP client configurations can be updated atomically with the code change.

objections.description: No strategy for handling absolute imports (e.g., 'from src.backend.ingest.parser import X' or 'from src.backend.search.index import Y'). When packages move to src/backend/, these imports become 'from src.backend.ingest.parser import X' or fail entirely if PYTHONPATH isn't manipulated. Manual import fixing across the codebase is error-prone and likely incomplete.
objections.suggested_fix: Use automated refactoring (ast-grep, comby, or libcst) to rewrite all internal imports before or simultaneously with the move. Alternatively, add src/backend to sys.path in all entrypoints, though this pollutes the namespace.

objections.description: Compatibility shims that simply import from new locations (e.g., 'from src.backend.web_server import main') will fail because 'src' is not a package and won't be in PYTHONPATH when running from root. Additionally, if the shim file has the same name as the module it's importing, circular import errors are guaranteed.
objections.suggested_fix: Shims must manipulate sys.path explicitly or use runpy.run_module. Better: make them thin subprocess wrappers or use pkgutil-style namespace packages. Ensure shims are not named identically to modules they import.

objections.description: 'Validate after path updates' implies moving first and fixing later. This risks a broken main branch if validation reveals issues. MCP integrations are particularly brittle and may not be caught by unit tests.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement validation gates before any move: 1) Run full test suite on current state, 2) Create feature branch with moves, 3) Run tests, 4) Verify MCP server startup (actual process spawn), 5) Merge only after green. Consider using 'git mv' to preserve history during the move.

suggestions.description: Add explicit pyproject.toml configuration for src/ layout (packages = ['src/backend']). Without this, pip install -e . won't find the moved packages and imports will fail in installed contexts.

Add risk: 'tests/ directory structure'. If tests mirror the package structure (tests/src/backend/ingest/test_parser.py), moving packages requires moving corresponding test files or updating test imports. Include test discovery verification (pytest --collect-only) in the checklist.

Reconsider deferring infra/ and deploy/. If these contain shared utilities (e.g., infra/config.py) imported by packages moving to src/backend/, deferring them creates cross-tree dependencies that complicate the refactor. Audit imports from infra/ into src/backend/ingest/search before deciding.
